old_claim.note: Galleon Ship Design provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No galleon prerequisite remains because the Venetian evidence concerns arsenal-wide galley and warship production rather than Iberian galleon design.

source_shape.source_locator: Shipyard & Workshops describes prefabricated warship subparts and rapid assembly; the article's naval discussion centers on Venetian galleys, galleasses, and arsenal operations.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The production method is documented as a Venetian arsenal process for its own warship system, not as an application of Iberian galleon design.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A ship-production workflow can serve multiple hull forms, and the cited Venetian case neither requires nor traces its method through the graph's galleon node.

ontology_before: Venetian arsenal prefabrication was modeled as downstream of Iberian Atlantic galleon design.
ontology_after: Venetian arsenal prefabrication remains a shipyard production process independent of the parallel galleon-design branch.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: arsenal_standardized_ship_parts no longer directly depends on galleon_ship_design.
would_reject_if: A direct history demonstrated that the 1574 Venetian prefabrication workflow was adopted from and technically required Iberian galleon design.
short_reason: The scoped Venetian production system is not a galleon-design derivative.
